name: Radiocarbon Dating
description: Radiocarbon dating exploits the fixed atmospheric 14C/12C ratio in living tissue: when an organism dies, uptake stops and 14C decays with a 5730-year half-life, so measuring the residual 14C and calibrating against tree-ring records yields a calendar age.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Organism dies"]
    N2["14C uptake stops"]
    N3["Initial 14C/12C known"]
    N4["14C decays (t½ 5730 yr)"]
    N5["Measure remaining 14C"]
    N6["t = (1/λ) ln(N0/N)"]
    N7{"Within ~50,000 yr?"}
    N8["Calibrate vs IntCal curve"]
    N9["Beyond range: lower bound"]
    N10["Calendar age"]

    N1 --> N2

    N8 --> N10
